rulings.Case Title: ADAM SMITH ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday May 4, 2022, line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. (tentative ruling part 1 of 2) Respondent's Demurrer to Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Based on the allegations in the operative petition and judicially noticeable documents: (1) no decision within the meaning of Govt Code 66499.37 was made on March 2, 2021 ("conditionally approving the decision, subject to the Board of Supervisor's adoption of the written findings") and nothing on the record before the Court or in the authorities cited by the moving party shows that the unconditional/final decision subject to Govt Code provisions was communicated in any way to the Petitioner 90+ days before filing and service of the Summons and Petition on October 6/7, 2021. Assuming that June 29, 2021 was the date of the decision contemplated by Govt Code 66499.37 (without make such a determination at this time as there are no allegations or judicially noticeable documents indicating that Petitioner was informed of this decision and when), Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.(end of part 1 of 2) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM SMITH ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent City And County Of San Francisco Demurrer To Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification (For Tentativ Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday May 4, 2022, line 1. Respondent City And County Of San Francisco's Demurrer To Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification (For Tentative Ruling Only Part 2 Of 2) the Court finds that Petitioner is entitled to equitable relief for failure to timely file and serve the petition; it is apparent from the record before the Court that the delay in filing, issuance and service of summons was at least partially caused by the e-filing requirements and backlog in processing such e-filings. In other words, had the Petitioner been allowed to file the petition at the window on September 23, 2021, the petition would have been filed and summons issued on September 23, 2021 within 90 days of June 29, 2021. Since summons were not issued until October 6, 2021, it was not possible for Petitioner to serve the summons. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.(end of 2 of 2) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GAVIN G. HANLIN ET AL VS. SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO Motion For Leave To Amend To File Second Amended Complaint And For Reconsideration Following Sustaining Of X-Pests Demurrer To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday May 4, 2022, line 2. PLAINTIFF GAVIN HANLIN, CHAYTON HANLIN's Motion For Leave To Amend To File Second Amended Complaint And For Reconsideration Following Sustaining Of X-Pests Demurrer To Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to Amend to File Second Amended Complaint and for Reconsideration following Sustaining X-Pests Demurrer to Plaintiff's Amended Complaint is DENIED. The Court, upon reviewing the proposed second amended complaint, finds that claims for liability against the moving defendants are barred. Garcia v. Rehrig Intern., Inc. (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 869. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.= (501/CFH)
